sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA 109 of 2019 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection No.22. 2. For hearing of main case. 04.03.2026 Mr. Irfan Mir Halepota, advocate for the applicant. Matter is pending since 2019 without any progress. On 02.11.2020, following order was passed: “02.11.2020 Mr. Irfan Mir Halepoto, advocate for the applicant. 1. To be complied with before the next date. 2. Granted subject to all just exceptions. 3. Learned counsel for the petitioner requests for time to go through the judgments upon which the reliance has been placed by the Appellate Tribunal Inland Revenue, Karachi. On his request, adjourned to be fixed after four weeks.” On 09.12.2020, following order was passed: “09.12.2020 Mr. Baha Khan, advocate holding brief for Mr. Irfan Mir Halepota, advocate for applicant, who is reportedly out of city and requests for adjournment.. Adjourned.” Today once again counsel seeks time for no cogent reason whatsoever and remain unable to assist insofar as the observation dated 02.11.2020, It appears that the applicant is not interested in the present proceedings. Accordingly, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, reference application is dismissed for non- prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Khuhro/PS
